The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Apinagia batrachiifolia include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
